name: WinPEAP
description: WinPEAP is a WinPE-based workflow to transition devices to Entra Joined and auto-enroll them into Intune via Windows Autopilot. It uses OSDCloud to build a customized WinPE ISO, injects the 4kAutopilotHashUpload.ps1 script and oa3tool-based hardware hash capture, and uploads the Autopilot hash to Intune during WinPE. Automation spans OS deployment, driver injection, hash registration, and enrollment, with support for user-driven Autopilot profiles and VM testing considerations.
author: Beau LaWalt

securityCheck.aiSummary: The script is a legitimate WinPE Autopilot hash collection and upload tool, but it contains several security concerns worth addressing: hardcoded placeholder credentials (and a malformed AppSecret), a default behavior that may auto-upload without explicit opt-in, error handling that could disclose sensitive data, use of a beta Graph API endpoint, and handling of sensitive data in temporary files. Recommended mitigations include removing hardcoded credentials, providing secure means to supply AppSecret (e.g., secure vault or interactive prompt), aligning defaults with documentation, redacting error payloads, using GA endpoints when possible, and ensuring proper cleanup and access controls for temporary data.
